title: Change RerankerSpecter Dataset question key
body:
> [!IMPORTANT]  
> The `Update branch` button must only be pressed in very rare occassions.
> An outdated branch is never blocking the merge of a PR.
> Please reach out to the automation team before pressing that button.

# What does this PR do ?

Add a one line overview of what this PR aims to accomplish.

**Collection**: [Note which collection this PR will affect]

# Changelog

- Add specific line by line info of high level changes in this PR.

# Usage

- You can potentially add a usage example below

```python
# Add a code snippet demonstrating how to use this 
```

# GitHub Actions CI

The Jenkins CI system has been replaced by GitHub Actions self-hosted runners.

The GitHub Actions CI will run automatically when the "Run CICD" label is added to the PR.
To re-run CI remove and add the label again.
To run CI on an untrusted fork, a NeMo user with write access must first click "Approve and run".

# Before your PR is "Ready for review"

**Pre checks**:

- [ ] Make sure you read and followed [Contributor guidelines](https://github.com/NVIDIA/NeMo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] Did you write any new necessary tests?
- [ ] Did you add or update any necessary documentation?
- [ ] Does the PR affect components that are optional to install? (Ex: Numba, Pynini, Apex etc)
  - [ ] Reviewer: Does the PR have correct import guards for all optional libraries?
  
**PR Type**:

- [ ] New Feature
- [ ] Bugfix
- [ ] Documentation

If you haven't finished some of the above items you can still open "Draft" PR.

## Who can review?

Anyone in the community is free to review the PR once the checks have passed.
[Contributor guidelines](https://github.com/NVIDIA/NeMo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) contains specific people who can review PRs to various areas.

# Additional Information

- Related to # (issue)

step_plan.summary: Fix the SPECTER reranker data pipeline by aligning the reranker dataset reader’s `question_key` with the actual JSONL field name produced by preprocessing (currently `query`), optionally adding compatibility fallback and updating/confirming schema tests and related preprocessors remain consistent.

steps.operation: Switch SPECTER reranker dataset construction to use the on-disk JSONL query field key
steps.reason: `SpecterReRankerDataModule._preprocess_and_split_data` writes JSONL records with a `query` field, but `_create_dataset` currently hardcodes `question_key="question"`, causing `ReRankerDataset` to look up a non-existent key and fail at runtime. Setting `question_key="query"` aligns reader and writer without changing the data schema.
steps.side_effects: Fixes KeyError/incorrect parsing for SPECTER reranker. Any external code that relied on the (broken) `question` key for SPECTER reranker JSONL would need to use `query` instead, but this should only affect consumers of these specific preprocessed files.

steps.operation: (Optional hardening) Add backward-compatible fallback for question/query key mismatch when processing examples
steps.reason: If there are existing preprocessed artifacts in the wild that use `question` while newer code uses `query` (or vice-versa), a small compatibility shim (e.g., if configured key missing, try the other common key) prevents breakage and makes the dataset loader more robust across versions.
steps.side_effects: Could mask schema mistakes by silently accepting multiple keys; consider logging a warning when fallback is used. Might slightly complicate debugging if users expect strict schema validation.

steps.operation: Ensure SPECTER reranker JSONL writer and schema remain consistent with the chosen key
steps.reason: This function is the producer of the JSONL schema (`query`, `pos_doc`, `neg_doc`). Confirm it continues to emit the key that `_create_dataset` now expects (preferably `query`), and update any inline comments/docstrings to reflect the canonical key name.
steps.side_effects: If you decide instead to rename the emitted key to `question`, this will invalidate existing cached/preprocessed splits and require updating any tests and other consumers accordingly.

steps.operation: Keep BERT SPECTER preprocessing aligned with the canonical SPECTER JSONL schema key
steps.reason: There are two SPECTER preprocessors (GPT reranker and BERT SPECTER). If the project standardizes the query/question key name for SPECTER JSONL (e.g., `query`), this module should remain consistent to avoid divergent schemas across collections.
steps.side_effects: If changed, will require regenerating fixtures/artifacts and updating tests that validate the output schema. If no schema change is made (only reranker mapping), this step may be a no-op aside from verifying consistency.

steps.operation: Update schema assertion test to reflect the canonical key and prevent regressions
steps.reason: This test guards the JSONL schema produced by SPECTER preprocessing. If the PR changes the canonical key name (e.g., from `query` to `question`) or adds compatibility behavior, the test should be updated/extended to assert the correct expected key so future changes don’t reintroduce mismatches.
steps.side_effects: Test expectations will change; ensure CI covers both the preprocessing path and (ideally) a minimal dataset read path. If only reranker mapping changes (keeping `query`), the test may stay the same, but consider adding an assertion that reranker datamodule uses the same key to catch future drift.
